auth_notes: Authentication is performed by passing an 'apikey' parameter within the query string or request body of the HTTP Request node.
rate_limits: Not explicitly defined in public documentation; generally subject to standard SMS gateway throughput limits and carrier-specific filtering.
gotchas: The API documentation is relatively brief, requiring trial and error for specific error response handling.
Phone numbers must be strictly formatted in E.164 format, which may require a preceding 'Edit Fields' or 'Code' node in n8n.
Lack of a native node means manual handling of API response codes (e.g., 200 vs 400) to ensure workflow error triggers work correctly.
client_must_provide: API Key from the SMS Reminders dashboard
A registered and verified sender phone number or ID
research_notes: Integration will utilize the n8n HTTP Request node. Most operations use POST or GET requests to 'https://smsreminders.com/api/'. Developers should use the 'Header Auth' or 'Query Parameter' options in n8n's credentials manager to store the API key securely. Webhooks for delivery receipts must be manually pointed to an n8n Webhook trigger URL within the SMS Reminders user settings.

effort_recommendation.rationale: Integration will utilize the n8n HTTP Request node. Most operations use POST or GET requests to 'https://smsreminders.com/api/'. Developers should use the 'Header Auth' or 'Query Parameter' options in n8n's credentials manager to store the API key securely. Webhooks for delivery receipts must be manually pointed to an n8n Webhook trigger URL within the SMS Reminders user settings.
